content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) maintain the blueprint markers that require *semantic* judgement (`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations), and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	`\leanok` placement is handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs immediately before you. **Do not add or remove `\leanok` yourself.** If a `\leanok` you expect is missing, the underlying Lean source still has a sorry or doesn't compile — investigate, don't paper over.
6	
7	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs.** You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts as part of your analysis. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint-marker files.
8	
9	## Iteration number — canonical
10	
11	Your invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. As of 2026 these are the **same number** — `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN` (i.e. M == NNN). Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) when referencing the iteration in prose (`summary.md` metadata, recommendations titles like "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})") and the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory. If older `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` files use the legacy independent session counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the iteration number.
12	
13	## Step 1: Identify Context
14	
15	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
16	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
17	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
18	
19	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
20	
21	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
22	
23	The file contains:
24	
25	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
26	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
27	
28	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
29	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
30	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
31	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
32	
33	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
34	
35	## Step 3: Read Recent History
36	
37	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
38	
39	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
40	
41	Create the session folder and write two files:
42	
43	```bash
44	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
45	```
46	
47	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
48	
49	Must include:
50	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
51	- For EACH target attempted:
52	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
53	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
54	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
55	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
56	- Recommendations for next session
57	
58	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
59	
60	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
61	
62	```json
63	{
64	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
65	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
66	  "target": {
67	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
68	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
69	  },
70	  "session": {
71	    "id": "session_N",
72	    "model": "model-name"
73	  },
74	  "findings": {
75	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
76	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
77	  },
78	  "attempts": [
79	    {
80	      "attempt": 1,
81	      "strategy": "what was tried",
82	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
83	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
84	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
85	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
86	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
87	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
88	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
89	    }
90	  ],
91	  "next_steps": "..."
92	}
93	```
94	
95	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
96	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
97	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
98	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
99	
100	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
101	
102	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
103	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
104	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
105	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
106	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
107	
108	If your analysis shows the prover has hit the exact same blocker for several consecutive iterations on the same target, you should explicitly instruct the Plan Agent to avoid retrying the same approach without putting more effort into understanding the underlying issue.
109	
110	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md (Knowledge Base only)
111	
112	The per-session narrative (Overall Progress: total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, this session's analysis) goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` — see the next subsection. PROJECT_STATUS.md carries only the cumulative Knowledge Base:
113	
114	```markdown
115	# Project Status
116	
117	## Knowledge Base
118	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
119	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
120	
121	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
122	- <target>: <reason>
123	
124	## Last Updated
125	<ISO timestamp>
126	```
127	
128	If an existing PROJECT_STATUS.md still carries an "Overall Progress" section from a prior project that grew it, leave the old content where it is (do not delete history) but stop appending to it. New session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
129	
130	### Per-iteration sidecars (split write)
131	
132	Your invocation prompt contains a `## Per-iteration sidecars` block that names `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` as the destination for THIS session's narrative. Split your writes accordingly:
133	
134	- **The per-session "Overall Progress" narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**, NOT to PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Overall Progress" section. PROJECT_STATUS.md no longer carries a session log.
135	- **You DO keep updating PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Knowledge Base" section.** Cumulative non-obvious facts (errors not to reproduce, reusable proof patterns, Mathlib idioms that worked) still belong in the Knowledge Base. The Knowledge Base is the only growing-but-curated part of PROJECT_STATUS.md.
136	- **Format for `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**: same fields as the Overall Progress block above (Total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, plus any narrative analysis). Born-bounded — this file contains THIS session only, never a multi-session log.
137	- Older `iter/iter-MMM/review.md` files are on disk for you to read on demand, but the recent window is already injected in your prompt.
138	
139	## Step 6: Blueprint Markers
140	
141	`\leanok` placement is now handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs between the prover and you. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration in the Lean source, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. **Do not touch `\leanok` markers yourself.** If you see one missing where you expect it, the underlying file probably doesn't compile or still has a sorry — do not paper over that with a manual edit.
142	
143	Your remaining marker responsibilities are the ones that require semantic judgement:
144	
145	- **`\mathlibok`** (statement-block only) — declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias / direct reference). The deterministic script never adds or removes this; you decide based on the prover's task result.
146	- **`\lean{...}` macro maintenance** — if a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
147	- **`% NOTE: <reason>` annotations** — when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, add a `% NOTE: ...` comment explaining the obstacle so the plan agent sees it.
148	- **Stripping `\notready`** — if a `\notready` marker still sits on a block that the prover has now landed, remove it. The deterministic script does not manage `\notready`.
149	
150	### `\mathlibok` rules (your domain)
151	
152	Add `\mathlibok` inside the statement block when:
153	- The Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or a simple `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`), AND
154	- The Archon-side declaration itself contains no `sorry` and introduces no new proof obligation.
155	
156	If you add `\mathlibok`, no `\leanok` is needed on the proof block — the deterministic script will leave the proof block alone if there's no Lean proof body to verify.
157	
158	### Record what you changed
159	
160	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic script's `\leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]` and don't need to appear here):
161	
162	```markdown
163	## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
164	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
165	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`
166	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
167	- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\lean{Old.foo}` → `\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename
168	```
169	
170	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
171	
172	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
173	
174	**Always check the planner's iter sidecar for escalations.** Read `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` (this iter's plan-agent narrative). If the planner intentionally skipped provers — signaled by the `(no prover dispatch this iter ...)` marker in `PROGRESS.md`'s `## Current Objectives` section, by a "user escalation" / "hard gate fired" notice in the sidecar, or by the planner's `planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip` meta — you MUST write a concise alert banner to `TO_USER.md` naming:
175	- What the impasse is (one sentence).
176	- What options the planner has surfaced (one bullet per option, if applicable).
177	- Where to find full context (the sidecar path).
178	
179	The planner does NOT write to `USER_HINTS.md` for escalations — `USER_HINTS.md` is user-to-planner only. The escalation banner is your responsibility; you are the user-facing channel.
180	
181	**Rules:**
182	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
183	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
184	
185	## Step 7: Dispatch review subagents
186	
187	You may dispatch read-only review subagents BEFORE writing your consolidated summary. They are audits whose findings you incorporate into `summary.md` / `recommendations.md`. Each runs as its own fresh-context Claude process; spawning two or more in one assistant message runs them in parallel, subject to the global `max_parallel` cap.
188	
189	### Where the catalog comes from
190	
191	Your invocation prompt contains an auto-generated **Available subagents** section listing every enabled descriptor. Reviewers are typically marked `[read-only]`. A descriptor tagged `[MANDATORY]` for this phase MUST be dispatched before the phase completes. Do not `ls .archon/subagents/` — the catalog you were handed is authoritative.
192	
193	When you decide to invoke a specific reviewer, read its full prompt and directive shape from `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
194	
195	### How to dispatch
196	
197	Pattern (Bash tool, parallel-able, run **in foreground** so the report lands before you write your summary):
198	
199	```
200	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
201	  --name <subagent-name> \
202	  --slug <kebab-case-slug> \
203	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \
204	  --write-domain 'task_results/**'
205	```
206	
207	Each directive must be **fully self-contained** — the reviewer reads only what the directive points it at. The directive shape for each reviewer is documented in its descriptor under `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
208	
209	### When NOT to dispatch
210	
211	- If this session was a pure proof-filling round with no new definitions or refactors, skip reviewers. They add latency and cost for no value.
212	- If a previous session in the last 3 iters already ran the same audit on the same scope, don't repeat unless that scope changed.
213	- The plan agent may also have dispatched reviewers proactively — check `task_results/` for matching reports before doubling up.
214	
215	### Incorporating findings
216	
217	After the reviewers return, read every report. Land each finding in your `summary.md` and `recommendations.md`:
218	
219	- **CRITICAL / HIGH** findings → bullet at the top of `recommendations.md` with the suggested action.
220	- **MEDIUM** findings → bullet in `recommendations.md`'s body.
221	- **LOW** findings → one-liner in `summary.md`'s notes section.
222	
223	Do NOT repeat the full report content in your summary — link to the report file. The plan agent reads your summary, not the raw reports.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible
11	
12	/-!
13	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
14	
15	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
17	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
18	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
19	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
20	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
21	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
22	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
23	
24	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
25	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
26	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
27	
28	The companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (rank =
29	relative dimension `n` from `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`) is
30	deferred to iter-132+ for blueprint-RHS-pinning work; not in this file.
31	
32	## Status (iter-131 fix-up: pure-term body refactor)
33	
34	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
35	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
36	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
37	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
38	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
39	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
40	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
41	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
42	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
43	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
44	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
45	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
46	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
47	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
48	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
49	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
50	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
51	the outer expression is the explicit
52	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
53	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
54	binder), is preserved. The companion rank lemma
55	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` lives in a follow-up declaration
56	(not in this file); the iter-131 body shape is the testable deliverable
57	witnessed by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
58	
59	## References
60	
61	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
62	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
63	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
64	- Project infrastructure consumed:
65	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
66	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
67	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
68	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
69	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via
70	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`):
71	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`,
72	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
73	-/
74	
75	set_option autoImplicit false
76	
77	universe u
78	
79	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
80	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
81	
82	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
83	
84	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
85	
86	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
87	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
88	`ModuleCat k`).
89	
90	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
91	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
92	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
93	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
94	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
95	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
96	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
97	iter-132+ companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (in
98	a follow-up declaration) pins the rank to `n` from the
99	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
100	
101	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
102	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
103	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
104	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
105	
106	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
107	change).** The body is built as follows:
108	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
109	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
110	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
111	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
112	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
113	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
114	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
115	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
116	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
117	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
118	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
119	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
120	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
121	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
122	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
123	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
124	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
125	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
126	   `k`-module.
127	
128	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
129	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
130	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
131	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
132	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
133	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
134	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
135	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
136	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
137	the iter-132+ rank lemma can rewrite against. For the live consumer
138	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
139	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
140	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
141	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
142	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
143	
144	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The structural
145	properties (`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`, rank `= n`) are content
146	for the iter-132+ rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`; the
147	structural-shape accessibility needed by that lemma is witnessed here by
148	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`. -/
149	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
150	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
151	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
152	    ModuleCat k :=
153	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
154	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
155	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
156	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
157	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
158	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
159	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
160	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
161	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
162	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
163	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
164	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
165	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
166	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
167	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
168	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
169	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
170	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
171	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
172	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
173	    exact hxV
174	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
175	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
176	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
177	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
178	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
179	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
180	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
181	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
182	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
183	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
184	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
185	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
186	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
187	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
188	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
189	
190	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
191	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
192	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but
193	the outer `extendScalars`/`ModuleCat.of`-of-`Ω` form is exposed. This proves
194	that the iter-131 body refactor closed the iter-130 opacity defect: a
195	downstream rank lemma can `simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars]`
196	(combined with a `Classical.choose`-chain `obtain` on the same existential)
197	to rewrite the body to the explicit form. -/
198	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
200	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
201	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
202	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
203	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
204	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
205	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
206	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
207	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
208	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
209	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
210	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
211	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
212	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
213	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
214	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
215	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
216	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
217	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
218	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
219	  exact hxV
220	
221	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
222	
223	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
224	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
225	`finrank` equal to `n`.
226	
227	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
228	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
229	
230	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
231	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
232	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
233	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
234	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
235	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
236	
237	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
238	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
239	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
240	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
241	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
242	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
243	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
244	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
245	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
246	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
247	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
248	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
249	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
250	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
251	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
252	  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
253	  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
254	  set e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
255	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
256	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
257	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
258	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
259	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
260	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
261	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
262	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
263	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
264	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
265	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
266	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
267	    exact hxV
268	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
269	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
270	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
271	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
272	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
273	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
274	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
275	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
276	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
277	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
278	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
279	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
280	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
281	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
282	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
283	
284	end AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj

99	## Directive questions (Q(a), Q(b), Q(c))
100	
101	**Q(a) — Does the 5-iter piece-(i.a) empirical cost still constitute net-positive over-k commitment vs over-`k̄`?**
102	
103	PARTIAL. The strategy honestly admits LOC savings 0–500 (lower-bound zero) vs over-`k̄`+M2.c, with the qualitative case (single-chapter blueprint + revert wiring + iter-131 corrective tractability) carrying the decision. The iter-131 ground (iv) is the weakest link: iter-131 was itself corrective on iter-130's defect, so citing it as positive tractability evidence is bootstrapping. The actual decisive validation event is the iter-132 rank-lemma close. RECOMMENDATION: strike ground (iv) from the over-k defense until iter-132 closes. The over-k commitment then rests on quantitative parity (lower-bound zero) + qualitative cleanliness (ii) + revert wiring (iii). At iter-132 close, if the rank lemma closes, ground (iv) can be reinstated as iter-132 (NOT iter-131) tractability evidence — but the empirical 5-iter piece-(i.a) cost should be benchmarked against a counterfactual over-`k̄`-baseline 3-iter / ~400 LOC budget. If the over-k path's piece (i.a) ran more than 50% above counterfactual over-`k̄`, formally re-open the decision.
104	
105	**Q(b) — Is the iter-132 rank-lemma-close fibre-free evaluation criterion sound, or should it fire unconditionally?**
106	
107	The current criterion "if rank lemma closes ≤ 100 LOC at iter-132, evaluate fibre-free for (i.b)+(i.c)" is logically inverted: cheap rank-lemma close is *validation* of (B), not evidence to pivot. The fibre-free elevation should fire UNCONDITIONALLY at iter-132 close, comparing projected (i.b)+(i.c) LOC under (B) (200–500 + 100–300 = 300–800 LOC, per the sequencing table) vs projected fibre-free cost (~400–800 LOC bundled for (i.b)+(i.c)). Under the current strategy's own estimates, fibre-free is at the upper end of (B)'s bundled cost — i.e., approximately equal, NOT obviously cheaper — so the unconditional evaluation may well *return* "stay on (B)". But the evaluation should happen on the merits, not behind a trigger that fires when (B) succeeds. RECOMMENDATION: re-state the criterion as "evaluate fibre-free at iter-132 close regardless of rank-lemma LOC; pivot if projected (i.b)+(i.c) under (B) is materially larger than projected fibre-free cost, where 'materially' = >20% LOC differential".
108	
109	**Q(c) — Does Step 3 (`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`) cleanly unblock Step 5 (`Module.finrank_baseChange`) for the rank lemma?**
110	
111	PARTIAL. The chain is feasible but the analogist's "[verified]" gloss understates one wrinkle:
112	
113	- `_eq_extendScalars` provides `∃ U V e htop, cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G = (extendScalars …).obj (of … Ω[…])`. The witnesses U, V are bound by the existential; the freeness / rank data of `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` is NOT carried by this lemma.
114	- The rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` must therefore re-extract freeness via a parallel call to `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀`. Because this is the *same* function call as the def's `let h := …` (and as `_eq_extendScalars`'s internal `let h := …`), the `Classical.choose` chains are *definitionally* equal — so the rank lemma can `obtain ⟨U, V, e, htop, h_body⟩ := _eq_extendScalars` and *also* `let h := smooth_locally_free_omega …` and extract the standard-smooth + base-change witness from `h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2`, with the two extractions matching by definitional reduction. This is the ~20–50 LOC of `Classical.choose` wrangling that the analogist's chain doesn't surface.
115	- Then `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential` (verified) gives `Module.finrank Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)] = n`, and `Module.finrank_baseChange` (verified) pushes this through `extendScalars`.
116	
117	The chain is plausible. The chain is not 1-step `simp only [_eq_extendScalars]`; it requires careful `Classical.choose` matching. RECOMMENDATION: the iter-132 plan should explicitly anticipate the parallel-extraction proof shape rather than trusting `_eq_extendScalars` alone.
118

130	
131	A fresh mathematician would approve the strategy as *mathematically* sound — the over-k decomposition into (i)+(ii)+(iii) is correct; the Replacement (B) chart-base-change body is structurally accessible; the genus-stratified body restructure is well-formed; M3 is honestly framed as multi-month off-critical-path; the no-new-axiom + zero-sorry end-state is consistent with the protected signatures.

133	But a fresh mathematician would push back on the *strategic posture* in three places: (1) the over-k commitment is now defended by qualitative grounds after quantitative parity collapsed to lower-bound zero, with the iter-131 corrective tractability ground (iv) being bootstrapped before iter-132 validates it; (2) the fibre-free criterion is logically inverted — it triggers when (B) succeeds, which is bizarre for a "pivot away from (B)" criterion; and (3) the strategy commits 800–1500 LOC to scheme-level absolute Frobenius (piece (iii)) without weighing the no-Frobenius / higher-Kähler-vanishing alternative, violating the strategy's own iter-128 user-hint citation discipline.

## Metadata
- **Iteration**: 132 (canonical)

- **Stage**: prover
- **Sorry count before**: 3 (`Jacobian.lean:188`, `Jacobian.lean:213` (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`), `RigidityKbar.lean:87`)
- **Sorry count after**: 3 (`Jacobian.lean:192`, `Jacobian.lean:213`, `RigidityKbar.lean:87`)
- **Targets attempted**: 1 substantive (`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`)
- **Prover wall-clock**: 347s (≈ 5.8 min)

- **Compile status**: clean. `lean_diagnostic_messages` = `[]`. `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` exits silently.
- **Axiom posture**: `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` returns `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` — kernel-only, no `sorryAx`, no project axioms.

## Headline outcome

**META-PATTERN TRIPWIRE PASSED.** Iter-132 was the iter-128/129/130/131 4-iter watch on `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`: would the iter-131 `Classical.choose`-chain body shape unblock a sorry-elimination dispatch (the rank lemma) without surfacing a third opacity-class defect? **Yes.** The prover closed the new theorem `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` in 1 substantive Edit (a ~40-LOC proof block placed inline at line 244 of the same file) plus 1 style edit (`show` → `change` to silence the Lean linter on the carrier-shifting tactic). Per `progress-critic-iter132`'s META-PATTERN tripwire framing, Route 1 (`Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`) **flips from CHURNING to CONVERGING** with this iter.

The closure consumes the iter-131 strong acceptance lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` *implicitly* via the alternative "parallel-extraction" route: rather than `rw [_eq_extendScalars]`-ing the body to the explicit form, the proof reconstructs the same `Classical.choose`-chain that the body uses (`let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega …; set U := h.choose; …`) so the goal's carrier `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` and the proof's TensorProduct expression `TensorProduct Γ(G,V) k Ω[…]` reduce definitionally via `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'`. `change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct …) = n` is the bridging tactic. The proof then closes in two steps: `rw [Module.finrank_baseChange]` pushes finrank from `k`-side to `Γ(G,V)`-side; `exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` consumes the existential's rank witness.

## Per-target detail

### `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` — SOLVED

**Location**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:244–282` (new this iter).

**Goal closed**:
```
Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n
```
under `[Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] {n : ℕ}
       [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]`.

**Attempt 1 (first Edit)**: drafted the full ~40-LOC proof body using:
- `let`/`set` to extract `U, V, e, hxV, hfree, hrank` via the parallel
  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀`.
- `letI` for the `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G.left, V)` algebra structure (matches the body's `letI`).
- `letI` for the `Γ(G.left, V) → k` algebra structure (induced by the
  `ψV` ring map composed via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`).
- `haveI : Nontrivial Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial` to discharge the
  `StrongRankCondition Γ(G.left, V)` instance hypothesis of `Module.finrank_baseChange`
  via Mathlib's `commRing_strongRankCondition` instance.
- `show … = n` to expose the underlying `TensorProduct` carrier via
  `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'`'s definitional unfolding.
- `rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := Γ(G.left, V)) (M' := Ω[…])]`
  to push finrank from `k`-side to `Γ(G.left, V)`-side, discharging
  `Module.Free Γ(G.left, V) Ω[…]` via `hfree`.
- `exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to consume `hrank : Module.rank … = ↑n`.

**Error encountered**: Lean linter warning on the `show` tactic:
> The `show` tactic should only be used to indicate intermediate goal states for readability. However, this tactic invocation changed the goal.

**Attempt 2 (second Edit)**: replaced `show` with `change`. Both tactics share the
same defeq-on-goal semantics but Lean treats them differently for style — `show` is
meant for restating the goal verbatim; `change` is the right primitive when the
goal's carrier shifts to a definitionally-equal form (here via
`ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'` reducing to `TensorProduct …`). Warning cleared.

**Final status**: COMPLETE; sorry-count delta 0 (the new theorem has no `sorry`);
project sorry total unchanged at 3 (the close is a *new* declaration, not a
closure of an existing `sorry`-bodied scaffold).

### `genusZeroWitness` — NOT ATTEMPTED (intentional defer)

Iter-127 scaffold sorry at `Jacobian.lean:192`. Per STRATEGY.md, body closure
scheduled iter-138+ when the cotangent-vanishing pile closes. Off-limits this
iter.

Three narrow items absorbed via Wave-3 parallel writer `rigiditykbar-uses-cleanup-iter132`. `Jacobian.tex` drift deferred iter-133+ informational. |

## Reusable proof patterns discovered iter-132 (new KB entries below)

1. **`Classical.choose`-chain parallel extraction for rank lemmas against opaque body shapes.** When the body of a `noncomputable def` is built from a `Classical.choose`-chain on an existential `h : ∃ X, P X`, a downstream rank-style lemma whose goal targets the body's underlying carrier (e.g. `Module.finrank k (someDef G) = n`) can be proven by **reconstructing the same `Classical.choose`-chain locally inside the lemma's proof**. Because the same `Classical.choose` call (with same arguments) produces the *same* term (Lean is definitional on `Classical.choose`-applied-to-same-prop-term), the goal's `someDef G` and the proof's `(extendScalars ψ).obj (of … Ω[…])` form share the same underlying carrier — exposable via `change` to the explicit form. This avoids the cost of building a `_eq_extendScalars`-style strong acceptance lemma + `obtain` + `rw [heq]` chain (which the iter-131 refactor also installs as an alternative). Key insight: `Classical.choose` is opaque but *deterministic on equal proofs*; two `Classical.choose`-chains on the same existential reduce to definitionally-equal terms.

2. **`RingHom.domain_nontrivial` discharges `Nontrivial` instance on the source of a ring map into a field.** When `Module.finrank_baseChange` (or similar Mathlib lemmas) requires `[Nontrivial S]` on the source ring `S` of a base-change `S → R`, and the project context provides only a ring map `S → R` into a (nontrivial) field `R`, the discharge is `haveI : Nontrivial S := (someRingMap).domain_nontrivial` (or `…hom.domain_nontrivial`). Combined with Mathlib's `commRing_strongRankCondition` instance, this completes the `StrongRankCondition S` hypothesis chain for `Module.finrank_baseChange`. Much cleaner than going through `IsAffineOpen V` of nonempty affines.

3. **`change` vs `show` for definitional-equality carrier shifts.** When a goal's carrier (e.g. `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G`) is *definitionally equal* but not syntactically equal to a target form (e.g. `TensorProduct Γ(G,V) k Ω[…]`), use `change` rather than `show`. The Lean linter warns on `show` when it observes the tactic actually changing the goal rather than just restating it for readability. `change` is the right primitive for genuine def-eq carrier shifts; `show` is reserved for verbatim restatement.

224	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
225	
226	- **`Classical.choice (α := X) ?_` is the standard escape for "Type-valued `def` body depending on a `Prop`-level existential", but it discards content**
227	  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical — surfaced as the critical iter-130
228	  finding flagged by `lean-auditor-review130` must-fix #1)*:
229	  When a `def` returning a `Type` (e.g. `ModuleCat k`) needs to use data
230	  from a `Prop`-level existential `∃ U V, P U V`, the elaborator
231	  forbids `obtain ⟨U, V, h⟩ := proof` directly with
232	  `recursor Exists.casesOn can only eliminate into Prop`. The standard
233	  workaround:
234	  ```lean
235	  refine Classical.choice (α := X) ?_
236	  obtain ⟨U, V, h⟩ := proof  -- now legal: Prop goal context Nonempty X
237	  exact ⟨theTermYouWant U V h⟩
238	  ```
239	  **The result is NOT definitionally equal to `theTermYouWant U V h`** —
240	  it is some unspecified inhabitant of `X` chosen by `Classical.choice`.
241	  Only `Nonempty X` is structurally recoverable; any downstream lemma
242	  about the structure of the inhabitant CANNOT be proved by unfolding
243	  the `def` and applying a fact about `theTermYouWant U V h`. **Use
244	  `Classical.indefiniteDescription` / chained `Classical.choose` /
245	  `Classical.choose_spec`** if any downstream proof needs to access
246	  the destructured data. Iter-130 instance:
247	  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` body wrapped the
248	  explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module
249	  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…⁄…])` in
250	  `Classical.choice (α := ModuleCat k)`. The body type-checks, passes
251	  `lean_verify` with kernel-only axioms, AND satisfies the
252	  progress-critic's vacuity-rule-out acceptance test (references
253	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`, 40 LOC, not simp-only) — yet the
254	  iter-129+ rank lemma `finrank cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G = n` is
255	  **unprovable against this body** for the same structural reason
256	  iter-128's was. **Lesson**: in addition to "kernel-clean ≠
257	  mathematically correct" (iter-129 lesson #2), kernel-clean +
258	  acceptance-test-pass also do not guarantee structural-content-
259	  delivery to downstream consumers. A `Type`-valued `def` whose body
260	  uses an outer `Classical.choice` wrapper is opaque to definitional
261	  unfolding; rank/free/finite/dimension claims do not transport
262	  through it. **Documented at
263	  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md` and §
264	  recommendations.md CRITICAL block.**
265	
266	- **Lattice symbols (`⊤`, `⊥`, `⊓`, `⊔`) cannot be part of identifiers in tactic blocks**

input.new_string:
## Knowledge Base

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **`Classical.choose`-chain parallel extraction for rank lemmas against opaque body shapes**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical — the technique that closed
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` in ~40 LOC after 4 iters of
  body-shape work)*:
  When the body of a `noncomputable def` is built from a chain of
  `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choose_spec` extractions on an
  existential `h : ∃ X, P X`, a downstream rank-style lemma whose goal
  targets the body's underlying carrier (e.g. `Module.finrank k (someDef G) = n`)
  can be proven by **reconstructing the same chain locally inside the
  lemma's proof**. Because the same `Classical.choose` call (with the
  same arguments to `h`) produces the *same* term (Lean's definitional
  equality is reflexive on `Classical.choose`-applied-to-the-same-
  proof), the goal's `someDef G` and the proof's
  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])` form share the
  same underlying carrier — exposable via `change` to the explicit
  `TensorProduct …` form via `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'`'s
  definitional unfolding. Recipe:
  ```lean
  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
  set e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
  have hfree : Module.Free … := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
  have hrank : Module.rank … = ↑n := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
  -- (install algebra structures via letI, ψV via let, Nontrivial via haveI …)
  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct … k Ω[…]) = n
  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := …) (M' := Ω[…])]
  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
  ```
  This avoids the cost of building a `_eq_extendScalars`-style strong
  acceptance lemma + `obtain ⟨U, V, e, htop, heq⟩ := acceptance` +
  `rw [heq]` chain (which iter-131 also installs in
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:198` as a defensive
  alternative). **Key insight**: `Classical.choose` is opaque but
  *deterministic on equal proofs*; two `Classical.choose`-chains on
  the same existential reduce to definitionally-equal terms. This is
  precisely the structural property the iter-131 body refactor
  delivered (per iter-131 mathlib-analogist
  `analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md`); iter-132 is the closure
  test that proved the design works for downstream rank lemmas.

- **`RingHom.domain_nontrivial` discharges `Nontrivial` instance on the source of a ring map into a field**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical — closed the `StrongRankCondition`
  instance hypothesis chain for `Module.finrank_baseChange`)*:
  When `Module.finrank_baseChange` (or similar Mathlib finrank-base-
  change lemmas) requires `[Nontrivial S]` on the source ring `S` of a
  base-change `S → R`, and the project context provides only a ring
  map `(someRingMap : S →+* R)` into a (nontrivial) field `R`, the
  one-liner discharge is:
  ```lean
  haveI : Nontrivial S := someRingMap.domain_nontrivial
  ```
  Combined with Mathlib's `commRing_strongRankCondition` instance
  (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FreeModule.StrongRankCondition`), this
  completes the `StrongRankCondition S` hypothesis chain that
  `Module.finrank_baseChange` requires. Much cleaner than going
  through `IsAffineOpen V`-of-a-nonempty-affine reasoning. Iter-132
  instance: discharged `Nontrivial Γ(G.left, V)` via
  `ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial` where `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.

- **`change` vs `show` for definitional-equality carrier shifts**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical — Lean linter warning surfaced during
  the iter-132 rank-lemma close, resolved by swapping to `change`)*:
  When a goal's carrier (e.g. `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G : ModuleCat k`)
  is *definitionally equal* but not syntactically equal to a target
  form (e.g. `TensorProduct Γ(G,V) k Ω[…]`), use `change` rather than
  `show`. The Lean linter actively warns on `show` when it observes
  the tactic actually shifting the goal:
  > The `show` tactic should only be used to indicate intermediate
  > goal states for readability. However, this tactic invocation
  > changed the goal.
  `change` is the right primitive for genuine def-eq carrier shifts;
  `show` is reserved for verbatim restatement to aid readability.
  Both tactics share the same semantic effect (def-eq goal swap) but
  Lean treats them as structurally different. Iter-132 instance:
  `show Module.finrank k (TensorProduct …) = n` → warning;
  `change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct …) = n` → silent. Both
  closed the proof; only `change` is linter-clean.

- **`Classical.choice (α := X) ?_` is the standard escape for "Type-valued `def` body depending on a `Prop`-level existential", but it discards content**

180	\begin{lemma}[(i.a) Rank of the cotangent space at the identity]
181	  \label{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}
182	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq}
183	  % Lean signature stub (iter-130+ rank-lemma build target; depends on the
184	  % bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} plus the regular-local-ring
185	  % + standard-smooth rank-pinning Mathlib pieces named in the proof):
186	  %   theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
187	  %       [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
188	  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
189	  %       Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G) = n
190	  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
191	  \notready
192	  In the setting of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}, with $[\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,n\,G.\mathtt{hom}]$ supplying the relative dimension parameter,
193	  \[
194	    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; n,
195	  \]
196	  and consequently (since rank is preserved under $k$-linear duality on finitely-generated free modules) $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g = n$ as well.
197	
198	  \emph{Lean encoding note (iter-128 per \texttt{strategy-critic-iter128}; iter-129 rename).} The right-hand side is the relative-dimension parameter $n$ from the \texttt{[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]} instance, NOT a bare $\dim G$ (which has no canonical Lean function on \texttt{Over (Spec k)}). The mathematical content is unchanged because for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, the relative dimension equals the dimension of the cotangent space at the identity equals what informal prose calls $\dim G$. The Lean target name is \texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq} (iter-129 rename, replacing the iter-128 placeholder \texttt{lieAlgebra\_finrank\_eq\_dim}, aligned with the renamed (i.a) declaration \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}).
199	\end{lemma}
200	
201	\begin{proof}
202	  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
203	  \leanok
204	  We close the rank computation under Replacement (B) (the iter-131 chart-base-change body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}) using the existential output of \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} directly. The proof is in four steps: Steps 1+2 form the live closure path; Step 3 documents an alternative canonical route through $\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}$ that is currently deferred under Replacement (B); Step 4 carries the conclusion across $k$-linear duality to the tangent side.
205	
206	  \emph{Step 1: chart-side Kähler rank.} Let $V \subseteq G.\mathrm{left}$ and $U \subseteq \Spec k$ be the affine charts (around the image $x_0 = \eta_G(\mathrm{pt}) \in G.\mathrm{left}$) extracted by \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (project lemma \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}); the same charts are pinned by the body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} via the iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain and re-extractable through the companion lemma \texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars} (see \S "Iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain body shape" below for the recommended downstream rewrite pattern). The existential's payload, on the $\Gamma(\Spec k, U)$-algebra $\Gamma(G, V)$ (with the algebra structure given by the appLE ring map of $G.\mathrm{hom}$), supplies in one shot both
207	  \[
208	    \texttt{hfree} \colon \;\texttt{Module.Free}\;\Gamma(G, V)\;\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)\,/\,\Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}
209	    \qquad\text{and}\qquad
210	    \texttt{hrank} \colon \;\texttt{Module.rank}\;\Gamma(G, V)\;\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)\,/\,\Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)} \;=\; n,
211	  \]
212	  internally consuming the Mathlib lemmas \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). Converting the rank-as-cardinal equation \texttt{hrank} to a $\finrank$ equation via \texttt{Module.finrank\_eq\_of\_rank\_eq} (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Finrank}; for a commutative semiring $R$ with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}, $\texttt{Module.rank}\,R\,M = \uparrow n \Rightarrow \texttt{Module.finrank}\,R\,M = n$) gives
213	  \[
214	    \finrank_{\Gamma(G, V)}\,\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)\,/\,\Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)} \;=\; n.
215	  \]
216	
217	  \emph{Step 2: base-change to $k$ preserves $\finrank$.} By construction, the body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} is the base change $k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}$ along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained from the identity section restricted to $V$ (composed with $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$). The Mathlib base-change lemma
218	  \[
219	    \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange} \colon\;\;
220	    \texttt{Module.finrank}\;R\;(\texttt{TensorProduct}\;S\;R\;M')
221	      \;=\; \texttt{Module.finrank}\;S\;M'
222	  \]
223	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Constructions}; requires $S$ a commutative semiring with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}, $M'$ a \texttt{Module.Free}\;$S$-module, and $R$ an $S$-algebra with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}), applied with $S := \Gamma(G, V)$, $R := k$, and $M' := \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}$ — using \texttt{hfree} from Step 1 to discharge the \texttt{Module.Free} hypothesis — yields
224	  \[
225	    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; \finrank_{k}\bigl(k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}\bigr) \;=\; \finrank_{\Gamma(G, V)}\,\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)} \;=\; n,
226	  \]
227	  which closes the rank lemma on the live (B) path.
228	
229	  \emph{Companion freeness fact (downstream consumer; not on the rank-lemma critical path).} For downstream consumers that separately need $k$-freeness of $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ — notably \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free} of piece (i.c) — the typeclass \texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.instFree} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Free}) supplies $\texttt{Module.Free}\;k\;(k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)})$ directly from \texttt{hfree}. This is informational here; the rank lemma above closes without invoking it.
230	
231	  \emph{Step 3 (alternative canonical route, currently deferred).} An alternative closure via the stalk-side cotangent space $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ is available in principle and was the iter-128-era plan. The route is: (i) bridge from the chart-base-changed body to the local-stalk Kähler module via \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} (currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}; identifies $k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)} \cong \mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$, via a localisation-at-the-identity-stalk step composed with the standard $k \otimes_{R} \Omega_{R/k} \cong \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}$ identification — Stacks Tag~02G1 / Hartshorne~II.8.7); (ii) invoke regularity of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}; from smoothness of $G \to \Spec k$ at the closed point $\eta_G$, Stacks Tag~00TR / Hartshorne~II.10.3.B), with Krull dimension of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ equal to the relative dimension $n$ (Stacks Tag~02G1); (iii) read off the rank from the defining property of regular local rings, $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2} = \dim \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} = n$, packaged into \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}) and \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs}). This route is preferred only when a downstream consumer requires the canonical (chart-independent) stalk-side presentation; it is \emph{not} on the live closure path under Replacement (B) because the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} is \texttt{\textbackslash notready}. The footer paragraph below records the iter-131 strategy-critic verdict on this deferral.
232	
233	  \emph{Step 4: dualisation conclusion ($\finrank\,\mathfrak g = n$).} By the standard rank-equals-rank-of-dual identity for finitely-generated free modules over a field,
234	  \[
235	    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g \;=\; \finrank_{k}\,\mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k) \;=\; \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; n,
236	  \]
237	  delivering the consequent $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g = n$ on the tangent side.
238	
239	  \emph{Mathlib name summary (live closure path under Replacement (B)).} The closure pieces consumed by Steps 1+2 above are: \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (project lemma \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega}; supplies the existential $\exists\,U\,V\,e\,(h_{xV} : x_0 \in V)\,(h_U\,h_V : \texttt{IsAffineOpen}\;\cdot)\,(\texttt{hfree})\,(\texttt{hrank})$ in one shot, internally consuming \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential}), \texttt{Module.finrank\_eq\_of\_rank\_eq} (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Finrank}; rank-as-cardinal to $\finrank$ converter), and \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange} (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Constructions}; base-change preserves $\finrank$ when the source module is \texttt{Module.Free}). The companion freeness typeclass \texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.instFree} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Free}) is informational for downstream consumers (e.g.\ \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free}) but does not enter the rank-lemma proof. All four names are verified in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} by the iter-132 plan agent.
The rank lemma is now closed in Lean. The stale `\notready` on line 191 should be stripped per the prompt.
